anomalies.detail: square and dispersion_reduction Phase 4 runs used 50-image cap; baseline and deepfool used 500 images. Recovery rankings for square and dispersion_reduction are approximate — mAP50 values are not directly comparable to baseline.

square attack.params.eps=0.3 equals the configured maximum. Coordinate descent converged at the search boundary — the true optimal epsilon may be higher. Consider expanding eps_max beyond 0.3 in the next cycle.

jpeg_preprocess degrades mAP50 under attack for deepfool (-13.6%) and dispersion_reduction (-10.1%). bit_depth also degrades dispersion_reduction (-1.7%). These are real losses, not artifacts — jpeg compression appears to interfere with the model's residual signal under these attack configurations.

highlights: deepfool is the most damaging attack at 63.6% mAP50 loss — highest priority for defense improvement.
c_dog is the only defense with positive recovery across all three attacks (5.7–11.0%).
jpeg_preprocess consistently degrades performance under attack; its ranking as a top defense in Phase 2 may reflect smoke-run artifact rather than full-dataset behavior.
square and dispersion_reduction image count mismatch means their damage percentages are approximate — treat 39.5% and 60.3% as directional, not precise.
